findings.message: Script tag injected from a remote origin at runtime

scanners.error: scanner timed out after 180s on a 41 MB bundle

findings.label: Weakened content security policy
findings.summary: extension_pages relaxes the default CSP with 'unsafe-eval', which Chrome Web Store rejects for MV3 submissions.
findings.remediation: Remove 'unsafe-eval' and precompile any dynamic rules at build time.

findings.label: Broad host permissions
findings.summary: <all_urls> grants read/write access to every site the user visits and triggers a manual review.
findings.remediation: Narrow to the origins you actually need, or move to the optional_host_permissions + activeTab pattern.

complianceReview.diffNote: Manifest diff against the last published version was skipped — pass --extension-id to enable it.

findings.title: CSP allows 'unsafe-eval'
findings.detail: Manifest V3 forbids remotely-hosted and dynamically-evaluated code. A manifest that relaxes script-src with 'unsafe-eval' is rejected automatically, before a human reviewer looks at the listing.
findings.recommendation: Drop 'unsafe-eval' and compile the rules engine ahead of time, or ship a WASM/JSON interpreter instead of eval().
findings.citation: Chrome Web Store — Manifest V3 remote code policy

findings.title: Update adds a permission that disables the extension for existing users
findings.detail: Adding "history" to an already-published extension puts it in a disabled state until every existing user manually re-grants the permission. Historically this costs a large share of the active install base.
findings.recommendation: Move "history" to optional_permissions and request it at runtime from the feature that needs it.

findings.title: <all_urls> host permission triggers manual review
findings.detail: Extensions requesting access to all sites are routed to a manual reviewer, typically adding days to the review queue.
findings.recommendation: Request specific origins, or use activeTab for click-driven access.

findings.title: Large minified vendor bundle without a readable source
findings.detail: A 41 MB minified bundle with no source map slows down human review and frequently draws a request for reviewable source.
findings.evidence: vendor/analytics.bundle.js (41 MB, minified, no source map)
findings.recommendation: Ship a source map or an unminified build alongside the artifact.
findings.citation: Chrome Web Store — code readability requirements
